text: Amarienborg
This is the home of the Danish royal family. It's a must-visit for its historical significance and the change of guard that happens at 12:00.
All week
Frederiks Kirke
Known as the Marble Church, it's a famous modern architecture.
Tuesday to Friday
The Little Mermaid Statue
This statue is a character from Grimm's Fairy Tales and one of the most famous landmarks in Copenhagen.
This is a world-famous restaurant that offers culinary masterpieces.
Rundetaarn
This 17th-century astronomy tower offers a panoramic view of Copenhagen.
Christiansborg Palace
One of the most significant landmarks in Copenhagen, both historically and politically.
Tuesday to Sunday
The type of power sockets used in Denmark are Type A and Type K.
The official currency in Denmark is the Danish Krone (DKK). Most places do not accept euros
There are in total 6 metros in Copenhagen. Visitors can reach anywhere by metro.
The official language is Danish, but English is widely spoken in Copenhagen.
The “hello” in Danish is “hej”, “thank you” in Danish is “farvel”, “goodbye” in Danish is “tak”.
Copenhagen's weather varies across seasons. Summers are warm, averaging 15-25°C, while winters can drop to -5°C. Always check the forecast before your trip.
